results.test_input:
Suggest 3-5 meals I can make with these ingredients.
Prioritise ingredients expiring soonest to minimise food waste.

- 2 lbs chicken breast (expires: 1 day from now)
- 1 cup rice (expires: 2 days from now)
- 500g broccoli (expires: 1 day from now)
- 2 cloves garlic (expires: 4 days from now)

I found several recipes matching your search on RecipeTin Eats! Here are the main results:

## Top Match: Chicken Broccoli Stir Fry

This is a big batch stir fry made with chicken and broccoli, with lots of sauce because broccoli florets hold lots of sauce but the sauce also soaks the rice. The sauce uses minimal ingredients but still matches real Chinese takeout, with Chinese cooking wine being a restaurant secret that adds depth of flavor.

The broccoli should be steamed or boiled until cooked to taste (it won't cook further in the stir fry), and the garlic should be chopped with a knife rather than using a garlic press since crushing makes it wet so it doesn't sauté properly.

## Other Related Recipes Found:

- **One Pot Chicken Broccoli Rice Casserole** - Made from scratch, this tastes like a risotto but is much more straightforward to make, and is a complete meal in one pot.

- **Chicken Stir Fry with Rice Noodles** - This is loaded with veggies and light on the noodles, making it nice and healthy, with varieties like Chow Mein and Beef Broccoli Noodles.

- **Chicken Fried Rice** - A fully loaded, completely delicious chicken fried rice that can be served as a side or as a main.

All of these recipes are from RecipeTin Eats and feature chicken, rice or noodles, and broccoli with garlic!


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Links: [{"title":"Filipino Chicken Adobo Recipe","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/filipino-chicken-adobo-recipe/"},{"title":"Adobo Rice","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/adobo-fried-rice/"},{"title":"Easy Chicken Adobo Recipe - Panlasang Pinoy","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/easy-chicken-adobo-recipe/"},{"title":"Killer Chicken Adobo - Panlasang Pinoy","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/killer-chicken-adobo/"},{"title":"Easy Chicken Adobo - Panlasang Pinoy","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/easy-chicken-adobo-recipe-knr-cc/"},{"title":"Chicken Adobo Fried Rice and Tortang Corned Beef - Panlasang Pinoy","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/chicken-adobo-fried-rice-and-tortang-corned-beef/"},{"title":"Chicken Adobong Puti - Panlasang Pinoy","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/chicken-adobong-puti/"},{"title":"How to Cook Adobo - Panlasang Pinoy","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/how-to-cook-adobo/"},{"title":"Not Your Ordinary Chicken Adobo - Panlasang Pinoy","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/not-your-ordinary-chicken-adobo/"},{"title":"Adobong Manok sa Gata - Panlasang Pinoy","url":"https://panlasangpinoy.com/adobong-manok-sa-gata/"}]

I found several chicken adobo and rice recipes from Panlasang Pinoy. Here are the main results:

## Filipino Chicken Adobo Recipe

This is a delicious and tasty marinated Filipino chicken adobo with soy sauce, vinegar, and garlic, best paired with warm rice. Bone in thighs and drumsticks are best, though boneless thighs work but cook faster.

### Serving with Rice

There are several ways to serve: plain steamed rice as the standard, hot rice with the sauce poured over the top, day old rice (which tastes a little sweeter with firmer grains and pairs well with chicken adobo), or garlic fried rice.

## Adobo Rice (Fried Rice Version)

Adobo Rice is a Filipino fried rice made with day-old white rice, chopped leftover pork or chicken adobo, toasted garlic, and oyster sauce. It is a fast way to stretch leftover adobo into a complete plate, often served at breakfast with a fried egg.

## Other Chicken Adobo Variations

- An easy chicken adobo recipe that is perfect for beginners and allows you to cook your own Filipino adobo at home with great results.
- Adobong Manok sa Gata is a version of Filipino chicken adobo that includes coconut milk. It is rich, creamy, and delicious.

You can visit the Panlasang Pinoy website directly to view the full recipes with complete ingredient lists and detailed instructions.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

args.prompt: Get the recipe name, prep time, cooking time, description, main ingredients, step overview, and the hero/thumbnail image URL from this page.

tool_invocations.result:
# Chicken Broccoli Stir Fry Recipe

**Recipe Name:** Chicken Broccoli Stir Fry (extra saucy!)

**Prep Time:** 10 minutes

**Cooking Time:** 6 minutes

**Description:** "Chicken Broccoli stir fry smothered with lots of homemade stir fry sauce! Truly takeout gold standard, this is a really quick and easy stir fry."

**Main Ingredients:**
- 500g chicken breast, thinly sliced
- 400g broccoli florets
- Light soy sauce
- Oyster sauce
- Chinese cooking wine (shaoxing wine)
- Sesame oil
- Cornflour/cornstarch
- Garlic and onion
- Water and oil for cooking

**Step Overview:**
1. Tenderize chicken breast
2. Steam/boil broccoli until cooked
3. Mix sauce ingredients
4. Cook aromatics (garlic and onion)
5. Cook chicken until done
6. Add broccoli and sauce, simmer until glossy
7. Serve over rice

**Hero/Thumbnail Image URL:** https://www.recipetineats.com/tachyon/2020/07/Chicken-Broccoli-Stir-Fry_2.jpg

tool_invocations.name: WebFetch

args.prompt: Get the recipe name, prep time, cooking time, description, main ingredients, step overview, and the hero/thumbnail image URL from this page.

tool_invocations.result:
# One Pot Chicken Broccoli Rice Casserole

**Recipe Name:** "One Pot Broccoli, Chicken and Rice Casserole"

**Prep Time:** 10 minutes

**Cooking Time:** 20 minutes

**Description:** A creamy, cheesy casserole made entirely in one pot that resembles risotto but is simpler to prepare. It serves as a complete meal.

**Main Ingredients:**
- Butter and olive oil
- Onion and garlic
- Chicken thigh pieces
- Flour
- Milk and chicken broth
- Long grain white rice
- Broccoli florets
- Mozzarella or cheddar cheese
- Dried thyme

**Step Overview:**
1. Cook onion, garlic, and chicken in butter and oil
2. Add flour and milk to create a roux-based sauce
3. Add broth, rice, and seasonings; simmer covered
4. Add broccoli and continue cooking
5. Top with cheese and broil until melted

**Hero/Thumbnail Image URL:** https://www.recipetineats.com/tachyon/2018/01/One-Pot-Chicken-Broccoli-Rice_7.jpg

tool_invocations.name: WebFetch

args.prompt: Get the recipe name, prep time, cooking time, description, main ingredients, step overview, and the hero/thumbnail image URL from this page.

tool_invocations.result:
# Filipino Chicken Adobo Recipe

**Recipe Name:** Chicken Adobo

**Prep Time:** 10 minutes

**Cook Time:** 50 minutes

**Description:** "Chicken Adobo is a classic Filipino dish where chicken is marinated in soy sauce and garlic, pan-fried, then simmered with vinegar, peppercorns, and bay leaves until tender."

**Main Ingredients:**
- 2 lbs chicken (cut into serving pieces)
- 4 tablespoons soy sauce
- 4½ tablespoons white vinegar
- 10 cloves garlic (crushed)
- 3 dried bay leaves
- 1 teaspoon whole peppercorn
- 1½ cups water
- 4 tablespoons cooking oil

**Step Overview:**
1. Crush garlic and marinate chicken with soy sauce for at least 1 hour
2. Pan-fry marinated chicken until golden
3. Sauté garlic until browned
4. Return chicken to pot with marinade, peppercorns, and bay leaves
5. Simmer covered for 30 minutes until tender
6. Add vinegar and simmer uncovered for 15 minutes
7. Season with sugar and salt to taste

**Hero/Thumbnail Image URL:** https://panlasangpinoy.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/05/Chicken-Adobo-in-a-Bowl.jpg

tool_invocations.result:
# Chicken Fried Rice Recipe

**Recipe Name:** Chicken Fried Rice

**Prep Time:** 10 minutes

**Cook Time:** 10 minutes

**Total Time:** 20 minutes

**Description:** "A fully loaded, completely delicious Chicken Fried Rice! Serve it as a side - or have it as a main!"

**Main Ingredients:**
- Oil (peanut, vegetable or canola)
- Eggs
- Chicken breast
- Garlic and onion
- Bacon
- Carrot and frozen corn/peas
- Cooked white rice
- Green onions
- Chinese cooking wine, oyster sauce, soy sauce
- Sesame oil and white pepper

**Step Overview:**
1. Prepare sauce and marinate chicken
2. Scramble eggs and set aside
3. Cook bacon with aromatics
4. Add vegetables and chicken
5. Combine rice with sauce
6. Mix in eggs and green onions
7. Serve immediately

**Hero/Thumbnail Image URL:**

metric_results.reasoning: The response strongly aligns with the expiry-prioritisation criteria. The critical ingredients are chicken breast and broccoli at 1 day, with rice also urgent at 2 days, and all three appear in expiringIngredients across the meals. The most urgent items are included prominently in the first two meals, both of which center on chicken and broccoli, matching the ordering requirement well. Coverage is high for critical and near-term items; the only minor gap is that garlic, which expires in 4 days, is used in meals but is never listed in any expiringIngredients array, so representation is not fully complete.

results.ground_truth:
Should return a JSON array of 3-5 meal objects. At least one meal must include
"chicken breast" and "broccoli" in usesIngredients and expiringIngredients, since they expire
soonest. All objects must have mealName, suggestedMealType, prepTimeMinutes,
cuisine, description, usesIngredients, expiringIngredients, and missingIngredients.

agent_config.name: meal-recommender
agent_config.description: AI-powered meal recommendation agent that suggests meals to minimise food waste

metrics.name: ExpiryPrioritisation
metrics.criteria: Evaluate whether the meal suggestions prioritise ingredients with the earliest expiration dates, as reflected in the expiringIngredients and usesIngredients fields of each meal object.
evaluation_steps:
Parse the input to build an expiry-ordered list of ingredients: identify which items expire within 1-3 days (critical), within 4-7 days (near-term), and beyond 7 days or never (non-urgent). If no ingredient has an expiry date, score 1.0 immediately — the metric is not applicable.
For each meal object in actual_output, examine the expiringIngredients array. Check whether the ingredient(s) with the earliest expiry date appear in at least one meal's expiringIngredients. Heavily penalise (reduce score by at least 0.5) if the single most-urgent ingredient is absent from every meal's expiringIngredients and usesIngredients arrays.
Count what proportion of critical and near-term expiring ingredients from the input appear across all meals' expiringIngredients arrays. A higher proportion yields a higher score.
Evaluate ordering signal: meals that feature the most urgently expiring ingredients should appear earlier in the array. Reward responses where the first 1-2 meals centre on the soonest-expiring item; penalise responses where high-urgency meals appear only at the end.
Apply the following scoring rubric and return a score between 0.0 and 1.0: (0.0-0.2): No expiring ingredient from the input appears in any meal's
  expiringIngredients or usesIngredients — the agent has completely ignored expiry.
(0.3-0.5): Some expiring ingredients appear, but the single most-urgent item
  (earliest expiry date) is absent from every meal.
(0.6-0.7): The most-urgent ingredient is present in at least one meal, but
  coverage of other expiring items is poor (less than half represented), or
  high-urgency meals appear at the end of the array rather than the beginning.
(0.8-0.9): Most expiring ingredients are represented across the meal set and
  the most-urgent item is featured prominently; minor gaps in coverage or ordering.
(1.0): All or nearly all expiring ingredients are represented; the most urgently
  expiring ingredient appears in the first 1-2 meals; the array ordering clearly
  reflects expiry urgency.

test_cases.name: Prioritise expiring chicken
test_cases.input:
Suggest 3-5 meals I can make with these ingredients.
Prioritise ingredients expiring soonest to minimise food waste.

- 2 lbs chicken breast (expires: 1 day from now)
- 1 cup rice (expires: 2 days from now)
- 500g broccoli (expires: 1 day from now)
- 2 cloves garlic (expires: 4 days from now)
test_cases.ground_truth:
Should return a JSON array of 3-5 meal objects. At least one meal must include
"chicken breast" and "broccoli" in usesIngredients and expiringIngredients, since they expire
soonest. All objects must have mealName, suggestedMealType, prepTimeMinutes,
cuisine, description, usesIngredients, expiringIngredients, and missingIngredients.
